authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC enforces the Funeral Rule and publishes funeral-provider guidance on price lists, itemized statements, embalming permission, preneed handling, misrepresentation prohibitions, and recordkeeping. This is directly authoritative for funeral arrangement documentation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Funeral arrangement document intake and consumer-disclosure evidence validation
used_for: GPL, CPL, OBCPL, and itemized statement evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating a funeral case file as complete when required Funeral Rule disclosure artifacts or itemized statement evidence are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / Federal Trade Commission
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for funeral-industry practices. It provides the binding baseline behind the disclosure, price-list, statement, and prohibited-practice artifacts the specialist must maintain but cannot adjudicate.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated funeral document classification and official-record boundary setting
used_for: authority boundary between documentation and legal compliance decisions
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors refusal rules for unsupported disclosure, pricing, embalming, and required-purchase claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Directors' Handbook on Death Registration and Fetal Death Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention / National Center for Health Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCHS is the federal statistical authority for vital statistics and the handbook is specifically addressed to funeral directors. It defines death-registration data responsibilities, demographic information collection, fetal-death reporting, and coordination with certifiers and registrars.
used_for: fetal death and death-registration artifact expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires death-registration status and source-of-truth checks before any disposition or certified-copy readiness statement.

authoritative_sources.title: Filing a Report of Death and Disposition/Transit Permit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state vital-record guidance explains funeral director/person-in-charge filing, medical certification, registered death records, death certificates, and disposition/transit permits. It demonstrates jurisdiction-specific workflow variation that must not be guessed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: State vital-record registration, certified-copy, transport, and permit handling
used_for: local registrar and state registrar status
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts exact state permit and certificate requirements into required tenant facts rather than universal claims.

authoritative_sources.title: MAVRIC Roles and Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state vital-record workflow guidance describes funeral home, medical certifier, burial agent, board of health, and city/town clerk responsibilities for creating, reviewing, permitting, and registering death records.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the specialist from treating EDRS access as authority to approve or amend the official death record.

authoritative_sources.title: Conference Model Disposition Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Conference of Funeral Service Examining Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Conference supports funeral-service licensing boards and publishes model disposition standards for regulators and practitioners. It directly covers authorizing agents, cremation and burial authorization, unique identification, chain of custody, and disposition recordkeeping.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Disposition authorization, chain-of-custody, and burial or cremation packet validation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes disposition authorization, identity, and custody-chain records hard prerequisites rather than generic document attachments.

authoritative_sources.title: Model Cremation Law and Explanation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CANA is a recognized cremation professional association and its model law directly names crematory records, authorizing agent, cremation authorization, identification, medical-device disclosure, and disposition of cremated remains concepts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cremation-specific authorization, safety, and release documentation
used_for: certificate of cremation and release of cremated remains
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports safety-blocker scenarios where cremation cannot proceed from documentation alone without authorization, identity, and hazardous-device facts.

authoritative_sources.title: SSA-721 Statement of Death By Funeral Director
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SSA form used by funeral directors for death-report information. It is authoritative for proof-of-death support artifacts and the limits of funeral-director reporting, but not for benefits eligibility decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Survivor notification and benefit-support evidence packaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Allows SSA packet completeness checks while refusing eligibility or payment decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Veterans Burial Allowance And Transportation Benefits
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official VA benefit guidance names the evidence used in veteran burial allowance workflows, including death certificate, DD214 or separation records, and itemized transportation receipts. It constrains packet assembly without granting VA benefit authority.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps veteran benefit support evidence-backed while routing eligibility, amount, and payment questions to VA or benefits specialists.

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records-management authority. Its ERM requirements define metadata, classification, retention, disposition, access, retrieval, integrity, and audit controls that govern sensitive case and official-record repositories.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Retention, access, metadata, audit, and records-governance handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires access, retention, legal-hold, and audit-log evidence before distribution, update, disposition, or handoff.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and jurisdiction classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify establishment role, jurisdiction, disposition method, facility or cemetery, requester authority, authorizing-agent posture, preneed status, and whether the request is documentation maintenance rather than approval or legal authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Arrangement and Funeral Rule document validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate GPL, CPL, OBCPL, itemized Statement of Funeral Goods and Services Selected, cash-advance items, embalming authorization, preneed references, and consumer disclosure evidence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Vital-record, permit, and registrar workflow validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate death-registration status, demographic worksheet, medical certification status, local or state registrar status, certified-copy handling, disposition or burial-transit permit, out-of-jurisdiction transport, and amendment routing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disposition, cremation, and cemetery evidence packaging
workflow_stages.description: Validate cremation authorization, visual identification, medical examiner/coroner release, identity tag, hazardous implant disclosure, custody transfer receipt, certificate of cremation, release of cremated remains, interment rights, interment order, grave or niche location, and cemetery register evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Benefit-support and aftercare documentation packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare SSA-721, VA burial allowance, DD214, death certificate, itemized receipt, transportation receipt, survivor contact, and aftercare evidence packets while routing eligibility or payment decisions outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, conflict, and audit closure
workflow_stages.description: Classify records, apply tenant retention and access controls, identify source conflicts, preserve audit logs, escalate legal hold or privacy issues, and close with traceable administrative status rather than official-record adjudication.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: At-need and preneed arrangement file, case number, family or informant worksheet, and authorization matrix
General Price List, Casket Price List, Outer Burial Container Price List, Statement of Funeral Goods and Services Selected, cash-advance item record, and embalming authorization

EDRS case record, death certificate demographic worksheet, medical certification status, local registrar status, certified-copy order, amendment request, disposition permit, burial-transit permit, and out-of-jurisdiction transport documents

Cremation authorization, visual identification form, authorizing-agent evidence, medical examiner or coroner release, hazardous implant or pacemaker disclosure, custody transfer receipt, unique identification tag, certificate of cremation, and release of cremated remains

Cemetery interment right, deed, lot or niche ownership, interment order and authorization, grave or niche location, opening/closing order, marker or headstone order, burial permit, sexton report, and cemetery register reference
Personal effects inventory, valuables release, body identification, transfer-of-remains log, refrigeration or holding status, embalming status, and chain-of-custody log
SSA-721 support, VA Form 21P-530EZ support references, death certificate, DD214 or separation record, itemized funeral bill, transportation receipt, and survivor contact record

Tenant SOP, retention schedule, access authorization, privacy flag, legal-hold flag, preneed trust or insurance reference, audit log, exception code, missing-item owner, due date, and record update note
systems_of_record: Funeral home management or arrangement case-management system
Electronic Death Registration System or state vital-record portal
Medical examiner or coroner release portal or document exchange
Crematory tracking, identification, and chain-of-custody system
Cemetery management, interment-rights, lot, niche, and marker system
Document management or enterprise content management repository
Preneed trust, insurance, and contract administration system
SSA, VA, or benefits-support document workflow where tenant-authorized
Records management, retention, legal-hold, IAM, and audit logging systems

allowed: Classify scope, validate completeness, and prepare funeral, cremation, cemetery, vital-record, benefit-support, and records-governance documentation summaries and evidence packets.
Determine whether available source records support blocked, incomplete, ready-for-owner-review, ready-for-handoff, exceptioned, or routed status.
Update only delegated metadata, checklist, attachment, status, exception-code, source-reference, and audit-note fields inside tenant systems of record.
Apply stricter tenant controls when they narrow but do not contradict the public baseline.
escalate: Jurisdiction, establishment role, disposition method, authorizing-agent authority, cemetery right, or EDRS/vital-record source hierarchy is unknown and affects required records.
Funeral Rule, state vital-record, cemetery, crematory, preneed, benefits, retention, access, or tenant policy sources conflict materially.
Death-registration, medical certification, permit, authorizing-agent, identity, custody-chain, hazardous implant, or medical examiner/coroner release records are incomplete or conflicting.
Retention, access, privacy, legal hold, public-record, certified-copy, or official-record questions require legal or records-governance interpretation.
refuse: Requests to backdate, delete, sanitize, conceal, or falsify official case, disclosure, vital-record, cemetery, cremation, custody, or benefit-support records.

Requests to approve legal right of disposition, cremation, interment, disinterment, body donation, death certificate amendment, benefit eligibility, payment/refund, or Funeral Rule compliance outcomes.
Requests that need legal, licensed, registrar, medical examiner/coroner, cemetery authority, crematory manager, financial-approval, privacy-owner, or policy-override authority.

domain_failure_modes: Treating funeral documentation as generic administration and omitting Funeral Rule price-list and itemized statement artifacts.
Certifying disposition readiness without confirming jurisdiction, EDRS status, medical certification, disposition permit, or medical examiner/coroner release.
Treating authorizing-agent paperwork as a legal right-of-disposition decision when competing next-of-kin or preneed instructions exist.
Approving cremation or interment from an incomplete packet that lacks identification, custody transfer, hazardous implant, or permit evidence.
Using payment, preneed, or cemetery lot records as proof of disposition authority without right-of-interment or authorizing-agent confirmation.
Disclosing death, cremation, medical, family contact, or benefit-support records without resolving requester authority and privacy/access rules.
Conflating death certificate, cremation certificate, burial permit, cemetery interment record, SSA-721, and VA support documents as interchangeable proof.
Overreaching into VA, SSA, insurance, probate, preneed trust, refund, or legal outcomes because the file has supporting documents.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific vital-record, disposition permit, cremation, interment, and record-retention rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Exact filing deadlines, permit timing, public access, cremation waiting periods, medical examiner release requirements, cemetery records, and retention intervals vary by state, locality, and facility type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, disposition method, establishment role, and tenant source hierarchy before final completeness or retention statements.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Right of disposition, authorizing-agent, and interment-right disputes
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Preneed instructions, written disposition authority, next-of-kin hierarchy, estate authority, cemetery lot ownership, and court orders can conflict.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat as required tenant facts and route disputes to licensed funeral director, cemetery authority, and legal reviewer; the documentation specialist only preserves evidence and status.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: SSA, VA, insurance, preneed trust, and refund workflows require different evidence and authorities, and approval outcomes are outside this lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Package evidence and route eligibility, amount, payment, refund, or trust decisions to the responsible benefits or financial owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Funeral home systems, EDRS, cemetery systems, crematory trackers, and document repositories may each hold partial or conflicting records.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant system map, retrieval hooks, source-of-truth hierarchy, and owner map before final record update or readiness claims.

conclusion: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because jurisdictional and tenant-specific variation is converted into required runtime facts, retrieval dependencies, refusal triggers, and escalation behavior.
